mnemonics: PRINT IS FOR PEOPLE: Python Really Is Not Trying to hide data; It's Sending Friendly Output to People
keyPoints: Use the print() function to display output to the console
Strings must be enclosed in quotes (single or double)
Variables can be printed directly without quotes
commonMistakes: Forgetting parentheses in print statements (e.g., print "hello" instead of print("hello"))
Using quotes around variable names when printing them (e.g., print(name) vs print("name"))
Missing quotation marks around string literals
